Looking at the code, I can see the issue. The problem is in `findNodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition()` on line 221:

```kotlin
private fun ASTNode.findNodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition(): ASTNode {
    val lastEnumEntry = children().last { it.psi is KtEnumEntry }
    ...
}
```

When the enum class is empty (has no enum entries), `children().last { it.psi is KtEnumEntry }` throws `NoSuchElementException` because there's no element matching the predicate.

Looking at `visitClass`:
1. It checks if `psi.isEnum()` 
2. Then finds CLASS_BODY
3. Then checks `lastTwoEnumEntriesAreOnSameLine()`
4. Then calls `findNodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition()`

The issue is that when the enum is empty, there are no enum entries, but the code still tries to call `findNodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition()` which calls `.last { it.psi is KtEnumEntry }`.

Looking at `lastTwoEnumEntriesAreOnSameLine()`:
```kotlin
private fun ASTNode.lastTwoEnumEntriesAreOnSameLine(): Boolean {
    val lastTwoEnumEntries =
        children()
            .filter { it.psi is KtEnumEntry }
            .toList()
            .takeLast(2)

    return lastTwoEnumEntries.count() == 2 && noNewLineInClosedRange(lastTwoEnumEntries[0], lastTwoEnumEntries[1])
}
```

This returns `false` when there are 0 or 1 enum entries (because count() != 2), so it doesn't prevent the call to `findNodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition()` in the empty enum case.


I'll modify the method to handle empty enums by adding a preliminary check for enum entries. The goal is to prevent the `NoSuchElementException` when no enum entries exist. I'll add a guard clause that checks if any enum entries are present before attempting to find the last one.

The approach involves using the existing `.takeIf` block to filter out empty enums, ensuring the subsequent logic only runs when enum entries are actually present. This method provides a clean, concise way to handle the edge case without complicating the existing code structure.

The key modification will focus on the existing predicate that checks for enum entries, ensuring it prevents processing when no entries are found.

content.text:
Now I understand the issue. Looking at the code in `TrailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRule.kt`:

1. The `visitClass` method (line 189-212) visits enum classes
2. It calls `findNodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition()` which calls `children().last { it.psi is KtEnumEntry }` (line 221)
3. When the enum class is empty, there are no enum entries, so `last { }` throws `NoSuchElementException`

The fix is to add a check in `visitClass` to skip empty enum classes. Let me implement the fix:

content.text:
All tests pass. Let me provide a summary of the fix:

## Summary

The issue was in the `TrailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRule.kt` file where `visitClass` method was calling `findNodeAfterTrailingCommaPosition()` on empty enum classes. That method called:
```kotlin
children().last { it.psi is KtEnumEntry }
```

When an enum class has no entries (like `enum class Empty {}`), this throws a `NoSuchElementException` because there's no element matching the predicate.

## Fix

I added a guard condition in the `visitClass` method to skip processing when the enum class has no entries:

```kotlin
?.takeIf {
    // Do nothing when the enum class has no entries
    it.children().any { child -> child.psi is KtEnumEntry }
}
```

This check is placed before `takeUnless { it.lastTwoEnumEntriesAreOnSameLine() }` to ensure we don't even attempt to process empty enum classes.

## Test Coverage

I created a new test file `TrailingCommaOnDeclarationSiteRuleEmptyEnumTest.kt` with tests for various empty enum scenarios:
- Empty enum class without body (`enum class Empty`)
- Empty enum class with empty body (`enum class Empty {}`)
- Empty enum class with multiline empty body
- Empty enum class when trailing comma is not allowed
- Empty enum class with only a semicolon
- Empty enum class with only functions (no entries)

All existing tests continue to pass, confirming that the fix doesn't break any existing functionality.

315â†’                            with(KtPsiFactory(prevNode.psi)) {

323â†’                                    add(newline)
324â†’                                    add(createSemicolon())
325â†’                                }
326â†’                            }
327â†’                            Unit
328â†’                        } else {

330â†’                        }
331â†’                    }
332â†’                }
333â†’            TrailingCommaState.REDUNDANT -> {

335â†’                    trailingCommaNode!!.startOffset,
336â†’                    "Unnecessary trailing comma before \"${inspectNode.text}\"",
337â†’                    true,
338â†’                )

347â†’    private fun ASTNode.getWhenEntryIndent() =
348â†’        // The when entry can be a simple value but might also be a complex expression.
349â†’        parents()
350â†’            .last { it.elementType == WHEN_ENTRY }

353â†’    private fun isMultiline(element: PsiElement): Boolean =
354â†’        when {
355â†’            element.parent is KtFunctionLiteral -> {
356â†’                isMultiline(element.parent)
357â†’            }
358â†’            element is KtFunctionLiteral -> {
359â†’                containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(element.valueParameterList!!, element.arrow!!)
360â†’            }
361â†’            element is KtWhenEntry -> {
362â†’                containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(element.firstChild, element.arrow!!)
363â†’            }
364â†’            element is KtDestructuringDeclaration -> {
365â†’                containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(element.lPar!!, element.rPar!!)
366â†’            }
367â†’            element is KtValueArgumentList &&
368â†’                element.children.size == 1 &&
369â†’                element.anyDescendantOfType<KtCollectionLiteralExpression>() -> {
370â†’                // special handling for collection literal
371â†’                // @Annotation([
372â†’                //    "something",
373â†’                // ])
374â†’                val lastChild = element.collectDescendantsOfType<KtCollectionLiteralExpression>().last()
375â†’                containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(lastChild.rightBracket!!, element.rightParenthesis!!)
376â†’            }
377â†’            element is KtParameterList && element.parameters.isEmpty() -> {
378â†’                false
379â†’            }
380â†’            else -> {
381â†’                element.textContains('\n')
382â†’            }
383â†’        }

385â†’    private fun ASTNode.addNewLineBeforeArrowInWhen() =
386â†’        if (psi is KtWhenEntry) {
387â†’            val leafBeforeArrow = (psi as KtWhenEntry).arrow?.prevLeaf()
388â†’            !(leafBeforeArrow is PsiWhiteSpace && leafBeforeArrow.textContains('\n'))
389â†’        } else {
390â†’            false
391â†’        }

393â†’    private fun ASTNode.findPreviousTrailingCommaNodeOrNull(): ASTNode? {
394â†’        val codeLeaf = if (isCodeLeaf()) {
395â†’            this
396â†’        } else {
397â†’            prevCodeLeaf()
398â†’        }
399â†’        return codeLeaf?.takeIf { it.elementType == ElementType.COMMA }
400â†’    }

402â†’    private fun containsLineBreakInLeavesRange(
403â†’        from: PsiElement,
404â†’        to: PsiElement,
405â†’    ): Boolean {
406â†’        var leaf: PsiElement? = from
407â†’        while (leaf != null && !leaf.isEquivalentTo(to)) {
408â†’            if (leaf.textContains('\n')) {
409â†’                return true
410â†’            }
411â†’            leaf = leaf.nextLeaf(skipEmptyElements = false)
412â†’        }
413â†’        return leaf?.textContains('\n') ?: false
414â†’    }
